concept: Bucket / Open Handled Container for Carrying
description: An open container with a handle, made to carry water or loose material by hand. Its body keeps shape while its handle takes the moving weight. 'lopha' (vessel) emphasizes what holds liquid; 'kolupe' emphasizes the repeated trip between source and use.
sound_symbolism: Firm 'ko' meets the handle where the hand does. 'Lu' curves around the load. Open 'pe' leaves the rim uncovered for filling.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'mia roe ha kolupe phialu kolua.' (I carry water with this bucket.) The next clause gives its contents, material, or capacity. Body shape varies within the open top and carrying handle.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A bucket makes household water use visible in the arm and the number of trips, even where a pipe later shortens the path.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Water and grain crossed the yard in handled vessels whose balance was learned by use. Ash or wash water made the same trip in the other direction.

tags.activity: carrying liquid or loose material by hand
tags.physical: an open rigid container with a handle
